items.name: GreenFrame
items.description: Estimates the carbon footprint of a user scenario on a web application. Full-stack analysis (browser, screen, network, server). Can be used standalone, in a CI/CD pipeline, and in conjunction with the greenframe.io service.

items.name: Cardamon
items.description: Cardamon is a tool to help development teams measure the power consumption and carbon emissions of their software. The tool is built around the concept of observations and scenarios.

items.name: CO2.js
items.description: A JavaScript library for estimating the carbon emissions of websites and web applications.

items.name: Website Carbon Calculator
items.description: Website Carbon Calculator estimates the carbon emissions of a website by analyzing factors like data transfer size and energy source of the hosting provider. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.

items.name: Ecograder
items.description: Ecograder analyzes websites for environmental impact by evaluating performance, design, development practices, and hosting, offering insights and recommendations to improve digital sustainability. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.

items.name: EcoIndex
items.description: EcoIndex evaluates the environmental impact of web pages by analyzing technical performance, complexity, and resource usage, providing a sustainability score along with actionable recommendations for optimization.

items.name: Ecometer
items.description: Ecometer is a tool that assesses the environmental impact of websites by analyzing their technical structure, performance, and hosting, offering a clear eco-score and suggestions to reduce digital carbon emissions.

items.name: Kastor
items.description: Kastor estimates the carbon emissions of a website and analyzes it according to eco design and accessibility guidelines.

items.name: Website Carbon Meter
items.description: Tracks the carbon emissions of a website as live metrics. Based on CO2.js and and the actual grid carbon intensity, provided by the Carbon-Aware-Computing project.
